text: Add a valueRationale field: a place to explain WHY an aim has its intrinsic value (esp. the root's value proposition)
description:
Today an aim has text + description + a bare intrinsicValue NUMBER, with no home for the reasoning behind that value. Felix (using Aimparency for a business-idea project) wants a dedicated place for the root aim's value proposition / "why this is useful", and more generally an explanation attached to intrinsic value.

PROPOSAL: a `valueRationale` field (free text, optionally structured) on intrinsic-value-bearing nodes. Fits the cooperation/legitimacy thesis directly — value judgments become inspectable and auditable (humans stay final authority over the value model). It also feeds the relevance check (8197b533): "is this still worth doing?" is unanswerable without the recorded WHY, and a structured rationale captures the assumptions a relevance check should re-validate.

SCOPE: schema field (shared types) + create_aim/update_aim MCP + frontend edit/display (root especially). Low risk, additive.

OPEN QUESTIONS (Felix, design): (1) every aim or only intrinsic-value nodes? (2) free text vs structured (list the assumptions the value rests on)? (3) does it influence the value model at all, or purely documentation/inspection (recommend: documentation only — do NOT auto-derive intrinsicValue from it; humans stay authority).

NOTE: the related cross-repo composition thread Felix raised (deep links repo+aim vs coarse repo-linking; normalized graphs compose by zeroing external intrinsics) is ALREADY covered by existing aims — do not duplicate: 5a45cb28 (cross-repo edge data model w/ repoId), de08d86a (merged cross-repo value flow, external intrinsics zeroed), 700e5b01, 1585aa46, a82b1530, f2e3dad3, c4df7232.

status.comment: Captured from Felix's design musing (2026-06-26). Design open questions in description; recommend documentation-only (no auto-derivation of value). Cross-repo composition part is separately covered by existing aims, not duplicated here.
